blocks.body: Present Perfect Continuous: Time Expressions — for, since, how long, lately, recently, all day, all morning, today, this week, and other markers of a process up to the present moment.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"Words like for, since, how long, lately, recently, all day, all morning, today, this week often point to Present Perfect Continuous because they show a process or duration up to now.","rules":[{"label":"for / since","color":"emerald","text":"These words show a duration or a starting point for an action that continues up to the present moment.","example":"I have been studying for two hours. / She has been working since 9 a.m."},{"label":"how long","color":"blue","text":"This is a typical question about the duration of a process up to now.","example":"How long have you been waiting?"},{"label":"lately / recently","color":"amber","text":"These words often talk about recent repeated or continuing activity.","example":"He has been sleeping badly lately. / We have been seeing her a lot recently."},{"label":"unfinished periods","color":"violet","text":"Words like all day, all morning, today, this week work well when the period is not finished yet.","example":"They have been working all day. / I have been reading this week."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common markers of this tense.","items":[{"label":"Duration","title":"for + period","subtitle":"for two hours / for a long time / for six months."},{"label":"Starting point","title":"since + point in time","subtitle":"since Monday / since 2022 / since breakfast."},{"label":"Recent time","title":"lately / recently","subtitle":"often used with repeated or continuing recent activity."},{"label":"Unfinished period","title":"all day / all morning / today / this week","subtitle":"the period is still continuing, so the process can be shown up to now."}]}

{"title":"2. How these words work","sections":[{"label":"For and since","color":"emerald","description":"For gives a duration, and since gives the starting point of a process up to now.","examples":[{"en":"I have been waiting for twenty minutes.","ua":"A duration up to now."},{"en":"She has been living here since 2021.","ua":"A starting point up to now."}]},{"label":"How long","color":"blue","description":"Ask how long an action or situation has been continuing.","examples":[{"en":"How long have you been learning English?","ua":"A question about duration."},{"en":"How long has he been working there?","ua":"A period up to now."}]},{"label":"Lately and recently","color":"amber","description":"These words fit recent repeated or continuing activity well.","examples":[{"en":"I have been feeling tired lately.","ua":"A recent continuing situation."},{"en":"We have been meeting a lot recently.","ua":"Recent repeated activity."}]},{"label":"Unfinished periods","color":"violet","description":"When the day, morning, or week is still continuing, this tense highlights the process up to now.","examples":[{"en":"They have been working all morning.","ua":"The morning is not finished yet."},{"en":"She has been studying a lot this week.","ua":"This week is still continuing."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common combinations","intro":"See how time expressions work with Present Perfect Continuous.","rows":[{"en":"I have been studying for two hours.","ua":"duration up to now","note":"for"},{"en":"She has been working since Monday.","ua":"starting point up to now","note":"since"},{"en":"How long have they been waiting?","ua":"a question about duration","note":"how long"},{"en":"He has been sleeping badly lately.","ua":"a recent situation","note":"lately"}],"warning":"Words like yesterday, last year, or ago usually do not go with this tense when the time period is already finished."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Mixing up for and since.","wrong":"❌ She has been working since three hours.","right":"✅ She has been working for three hours."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using a finished past time with this tense.","wrong":"❌ I have been working yesterday.","right":"✅ I worked yesterday."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Mixing an unfinished and finished period.","wrong":"❌ He has been studying all day yesterday.","right":"✅ He was studying all day yesterday."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["for gives a duration, and since gives a starting point.","How long is a typical question about duration up to now.","lately and recently often show recent activity or a recent situation.","all day, all morning, today, and this week work well when the period is not finished yet.","Finished past markers like yesterday or ago usually do not go with this tense."]}

{"title":"Present Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":true}]}
